title: China to buy U.S. oil to feed its 'insatiable appetite,' Trump tells Fox News
description: Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping are scheduled to meet Friday to close out a two-day summit that has featured pageantry and dealmaking.

title: Oil prices jump after Trump says China agreed to buy U.S. crude following Xi talks
description: Oil prices rose Friday on news that China has agreed to purchase oil from America

title: Billionaire Gautam Adani and nephew agree to pay $18 million in SEC settlement over fraud allegations
description: Indian billionaire Gautam Adani and his nephew Sagar Adani have agreed to settle a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission lawsuit over allegations they misled investors.

title: South Korea’s Kospi retreats from 8,000 milestone as Trump-Xi talks enter second day
description: Asia-Pacific markets fell as investors tracked the second day of high-stakes talks between Donald Trump and Xi Jinping.

title: Closing arguments conclude in Musk v. Altman, jury to deliberate next week
description: The jury's verdict in Musk v. Altman will be advisory, and deliberations are set to begin on Monday.

title: Figma has a fix for its ailing stock — a new way to make money off its AI products
description: Figma’s stock soars after earnings beat expectations again, and the full-year outlook was raised.

title: Obscure Japanese stock measure widens on global hunt for AI winners
description: Record gulf between Tokyo’s Nikkei 225 and Topix reflects major influx of foreign capital, analysts say

title: Is it time to worry about India’s foreign exchange reserves?
description: Also in this newsletter: government to invest $4bn in coal gasification

title: Fallout from China housing slowdown grinds on in offshore courts
description: Bondholders might balk at the recoveries offered so far, but any remedies involve complex negotiations

title: Hong Kong listings boom sparks legal hiring spree
description: The revival in deal activity has law firms rebuilding their teams

title: India Hikes Fuel Prices For The First Time in Four Years
description: India’s state-run refiners have raised fuel prices for the first time in four years, as New Delhi yields to crude prices pushed higher by the war in Iran, and to the resulting financial pressures battering its processors.

title: Fashion to Fish Farms: Meet Norwegian Billionaire Gustav Witzoe
description: 33-year-old Gustav Magnar Witzoe is a fashion influencer, model, investor and a Norwegian billionaire. He’s also betting his fortune on creating the “world’s best fish farm.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Comparing China and US Statements on Trump-Xi Summit
description: President Donald Trump's visit to Beijing has dominated Chinese state media, with news stories and commentary mostly striking a positive tone. Despite the grand ceremonies and glowing coverage, however, a comparison of summit readouts from the US and China suggests differences remain on key issues. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump and Xi Begin Second Day of Talks
description: Donald Trump met Xi Jinping in Beijing’s leadership compound on Friday, kickstarting a second day of talks. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Xi Sending Strong Signal on Taiwan, China Adviser Says
description: Fudan University's Wu Xinbo, an adviser to China's Foreign Ministry, says President Xi Jinping's statement on Taiwan during a summit with President Donald Trump was a strong and direct message urging Washington to halt arms sales and explicitly oppose the island's independence. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Chinese Steel Mill Profitability at Highest Level Since August
description: China’s steel mill profitability has climbed to the highest since August, according to data from consultancy Mysteel, although questions remain over how long the improvement can last.

title: Treasuries Lead Global Bond Yields Higher on Inflation Angst
description: US Treasuries slid with global peers as persistently higher oil prices threatened to worsen inflation and keep interest rates elevated for longer.

title: Romania to Hold Rates With Stagflation Risks Clouding Outlook
description: Romania is poised to keep interest rates at the highest level in the European Union as policymakers confront the dilemma of double-digit inflation and a deepening recession.

title: Copper Extends Retreat on Faster Inflation and Stronger Dollar
description: Copper extended its retreat from a record-high close as accelerating US inflation reduced the chance of rate cuts and a stronger dollar make the metal more expensive for many buyers.

title: Korea’s Kospi Races From 7,000 to Record 8,000 in Seven Sessions
description: South Korea’s benchmark stock index briefly breached the 8,000 mark for the first time on Friday, just seven sessions after it reached the 7,000 milestone.

title: Why Stock Rally Is Losing Momentum
description: A record-breaking global stock rally driven by AI optimism stalled in Asia on Friday, with sentiment souring across assets as global yields rise. Bloomberg Markets Live Executive Editor Mark Cudmore explains. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: India’s Growing Rupee Defense Reassures Stock Bulls
description: Nation is also mulling a steep cut in the taxes paid by foreign investors on its bonds .

title: Japan Producer Prices Jump by Most Since 2014, Backing BOJ Hike
description: Japan’s corporate goods prices surged in April by the most in 12 years, in another sign of how the war in Iran is boosting inflationary pressures and supporting the case for the Bank of Japan to raise interest rates.

title: JPMorgan Hikes Taiex Bull-Case Target to 50,000 on AI Buildout
description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. raised its targets for Taiwanese stocks for the second time in less than a month, recommending the market as “the most pure-play exposure to the global AI buildout.”

title: Yen’s Week-Long Slide Puts Traders on Guard for Intervention
description: Currency traders are increasingly alert to the risk of further market intervention by Japan following a 1% slide in the yen this week that has pushed it to 158 per dollar.

title: Trump Says China’s Xi Likes the Idea of Buying More Oil From US
description: President Donald Trump said his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping likes the idea of buying more US oil, as the leaders meet in Beijing.

title: US Expects Dramatic Agricultural Purchases by China, Greer Says
description: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said he anticipates that China would commit to billions in American agricultural purchases, as Presidents Donald Trump and Xi Jinping complete their summit in Beijing.

title: Greer: A Lot of Success in Rebalancing Trade With China
description: United States Trade Representative Jamieson Greer talks about the results so far from the Trump-Xi summit, saying the meetings have already delivered tangible returns. He speaks with Bloomberg's Annmarie Hordern in Beijing. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Greer: US, China Willing to Continue Trade Truce
description: Jamieson Greer, Trade Representative of the United States, signaled cautious optimism that US-China trade relations are stabilizing, with progress on agriculture purchases and rare earth supplies, though tariffs and supply chain issues remain. He speaks exclusively to Bloomberg's Annmarie Hordern, on the sidelines of the US-China summit in Beijing on "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade". (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Do gas tax holidays actually bring relief at the pump?
description: Trump wants to suspend the federal gas tax, but state-level gas tax breaks could have a bigger impact at the pump, says one expert.

title: Aging Palestinian Leader Boosting His Son’s Political Rise, Officials Say
description: Mahmoud Abbas’s years in power have been dogged by accusations of corruption. Many Palestinians yearn for fresh leadership.

title: Inside the Congolese Hotel Where Trump Deported 15 U.S. Migrants
description: They were shackled and sent to Kinshasa by the Trump administration. Now they face a dangerous choice: Go back to Latin America or stay in Africa.

title: Enter the Killer Robots: The Ukrainian Forging the Future of Warfare
description: Mykhailo Fedorov, Ukraine’s 35-year-old defense minister, sees futuristic military technology as crucial to his country’s survival.

title: The United States and China will start discussing A.I. safety, Bessent says.
What is Zhongnanhai, the Secretive Beijing Compound Where Trump Met Xi?
description: China’s leader, Xi Jinping, was meeting with President Trump on Friday at Zhongnanhai, a heavily guarded Beijing compound where top Chinese officials live and work.

title: China Starting to Fulfill ‘Promises,’ U.S. Trade Representative Says
description: As President Trump and China’s leader, Xi Jinping, meet, the United States said China has promised to expand its purchases of farm goods and airplanes.

title: Here’s the latest.
Australian Jews Testify on Experiences of Antisemitism in Bondi Beach Inquiry
description: As part of a public inquiry into the massacre at Bondi Beach, dozens have described being targeted for their Jewish identity in their daily lives.

title: After Trump’s pledge to ‘open up’ China, low expectations for trade deal
description: Washington and Beijing expected to extend trade truce, but analysts see only modest breakthrough in strained ties.

title: What happens to those who say no to fighting wars?
description: Al Jazeera’s @avawarrinerr explains what it means for people who object to fighting in wars today.

title: Israel-Lebanon talks held in Washington as expiration of ceasefire nears
description: Al Jazeera's Manuel Rapalo reports from Washington, where the first of two days of Lebanon-Israel talks concluded.

title: Eurovision braces for new protests over Israel’s participation
description: Eurovision is heading towards its grand final, but organisers are bracing for more demonstrations against Israel.

title: Iran war live: Trump’s visit to China shadowed by conflict with Tehran
description: Iran's foreign minister said Iranians 'never bow to pressure', and there is no military solution to issues with Tehran.

title: China’s hotel sector shines, poised to outperform through 2026 on strong travel demand
description: Several major US-listed global hotel chains reported strong performance in China in the first quarter, driven by robust business and leisure demand, with momentum expected to continue through the year. As US President Donald Trump makes a state visit to Beijing this week, international arrivals to China also jumped in the first quarter, fuelled by the country’s expanded visa-free entry policies for more countries. Four international hotel groups – Hilton Worldwide Holdings, InterContinental...

title: Mexico's President Announces the Resignation of Pemex CEO Victor Rodríguez
description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum announced on Thursday that Pemex CEO Víctor Rodríguez is stepping down, naming the state oil company’s chief financial officer, Juan Carlos Carpio, as his replacement. The move comes after months of speculation over Rodríguez’s future at Pemex, which has struggled with declining production, operational setbacks, and rising internal tensions. In a video address posted to social media, Sheinbaum appeared alongside Rodríguez, Carpio, and Energy Secretary Luz Elena González…

title: Trump’s big trip was supposed to sell 500 Boeing planes. China is only buying 200 of them.
description: Boeing’s stock on Thursday headed for its steepest fall in six months after President Donald Trump said China is buying 200 of the company’s jets, disappointing investors who had expected more than double that number.

title: This summer’s World Cup will be no match for Taylor Swift when it comes to live events
description: The World Cup runs from June 11 to July 19, with games being played in cities across the U.S., Canada and Mexico. Tickets went on sale in October and have sold for as much as $10,000, with the cheapest options to see the U.S. team running around $1,640.
